steps.title: Identify the main idea of the passage
steps.content:
The passage covers two things: (1) past studies hypothesized PSP was homologous (shared ancestor), and (2) Aureliano et al. found evidence against that hypothesis and concluded PSP “likely evolved independently in each clade.” The main idea must capture both the earlier hypothesis and the team’s contradicting conclusion.
  • "Aureliano et al. found openings in bones in fossils of common ancestors of pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades, but the team determined that the sizes of the openings were too varied to be indicative of PSP, thereby weakening the homology hypothesis.": Focuses on one detail (bone opening sizes) rather than the overarching conclusion about independent evolution. ✗
  • "Although past studies by paleontologists concluded that PSP in pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades was likely attributable to a common ancestor, those paleontologists lacked access to technology that led Aureliano et al. to reach a different conclusion.": Introduces the idea that past researchers lacked technology — not stated in the passage. ✗
  • "Previous studies hypothesized that PSP originated in a shared ancestor of pterosaurs and two other clades, but the findings of Aureliano et al. suggest that PSP evolved independently in pterosaurs and was subsequently inherited by the other two clades.": Misrepresents the conclusion: the text says PSP evolved independently in each clade, not just pterosaurs. ✗
  • "Aureliano et al. found that pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades with PSP likely lacked a common ancestor with the trait, leading the team to posit a different evolutionary origin for PSP than that posited in earlier studies.": Accurately reflects both the prior hypothesis and the team’s contradicting conclusion about independent evolution. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the main idea
steps.content: Aureliano et al. found that pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades with PSP likely lacked a common ancestor with the trait, leading the team to posit a different evolutionary origin for PSP than that posited in earlier studies. covers the full arc: earlier homology hypothesis + Aureliano et al.’s finding that PSP likely evolved independently in each clade. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
